備註: (n) a disease that exists in almost all of an area
(a) (of a disease) existing in almost all of an area

(n) a type of virus that causes diseases in humans and animals. In humans, it usually causes 
respiratory infections (= in the nose, throat, or chest) that are not serious
Ex: SARS was a new strain of coronavirus.
(n) the top rounded part of a body part such as a tooth or the skull,

(a) caused by a virus, 
Ex: Within days the film clip went viral

(n) a serious illness in which one or both lungs become inflamed and fill with fluid, 
Ex: The doctor checked whether the patient had pneumonia.

(n) any feeling of illness or physical or mental change that is caused by a particular disease
Ex: He's complaining of all the usual flu symptoms - a high temperature, headache, and so on

(n) a condition in which body temperature is higher than normal,
Ex: A high fever is a common symptom of infection

(n) to force air out of your lungs through your throat with a short, loud sound
Ex: The smoke made me cough

(n) extreme tiredness, especially caused by illness or hard work
Ex: Many patients reported fatigue after infection.
